steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that names the result of bursting forth - the dawn itself
Adding ال makes the word definite, like adding 'the' in English
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is being used as the object of a preposition

steps.m: night (the dark part of a day)

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic word for 'night'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple nights
Added the tanween kasra to show this word is being described by the number that follows it (عَشْرٍ meaning ten)

steps.m: and nights (being described by عَشْرٍ - ten)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic number word
Added tanween kasra to show this number belongs to or describes the nights mentioned before it
Added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'even number'. This word is mentioned alongside وَتْر (odd number) in the ayah.
The shadda (doubled letter) makes the 'sh' sound stronger, showing importance
Added the definite article 'al' to specify this particular even number
Added kasra at the end to show it's in the genitive case
Added 'wa' at the beginning to connect it with what comes before
Connected the root letters and added sukun to show it's a noun
Adding 'al' makes it specific
Adding 'wa' connects this to the previous word (وَٱلشَّفْعِ - and the even number)

steps.m: and the odd number (paired with وَٱلشَّفْعِ)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word is connected grammatically to the previous word وَٱلشَّفْعِ
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the night' instead of just 'night'

steps.m: and by the night (followed by إِذَا يَسْرِ - when it passes)
steps.n: Added و meaning 'and' at the start, and kasra at the end to show this is part of an oath. This word is followed by 'when it passes' (إِذَا يَسْرِ) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that indicates a specific point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to a conditional time in the future. Here it introduces what happens during the night, connecting to the verb يَسْرِ (it travels/moves)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and final vowel to kasra
Final ي is dropped because it comes after a word that makes it conditional (like 'if')
This is a question particle that always comes at the start of a sentence to turn it into a yes/no question
Added sukun at the end to show where the word stops. This word is asking about whether 'that is an oath' (ذٰلِكَ قَسَمٌ) in this ayah.
This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that shows location or position
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the ف to show how to pronounce it. This word connects ذٰلِكَ (that) with حِجْرٍ (understanding), showing that something is 'in' something else.
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a different way of writing the same letter at the end of a word. The meaning stays the same.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something nearby
The alif is written above the ذ to show it's pronounced longer
Adding لِ changes the meaning from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away
The كَ at the end shows you're talking to one person when pointing something out

steps.m: to divide, distribute, or make a solemn promise

steps.n: Adding the pattern فَعَل turns the action into a noun meaning the result or instance of that action - here turning 'to make a solemn promise' into 'an oath'
Adding tanween damma shows this is the subject of the sentence and is indefinite (not 'the oath' but 'an oath')
This is a special word used to show possession or ownership, like saying 'owner of' or 'one who has'
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' or 'to' the possessor
Added emphasis to the ل by doubling it with a shadda

steps.m: intelligence (from the idea of mental control)
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning intelligence/understanding, derived from the concept of mental control and restriction. Just like intelligence helps us control our actions, this word comes from the idea of restriction.
Added the -in sound at the end to show this word is connected to 'oath' in the sentence
Adding مْ makes the word negative and connects it to past tense
Adding أَ at the start turns this into a question that expects 'yes' as the answer
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to mean 'you' and changed vowels to make it present tense (you see now)
The word gets shorter because أَلَمْ makes verbs drop their last letter - like cutting off the end
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Adding a fatha mark at the end to complete the word

steps.m: he did (referring to what رَبُّكَ/your Lord did)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word shows what your Lord did to the people of 'Ad.

steps.m: to nurture, raise, or take care of

steps.n: The root letters ر-ب-ب became رَبُّ (Lord) by adding vowels and doubling the ب. The ُ ending shows it's the subject doing something.
The كَ at the end means 'your' (when talking to one male person). This word رَبُّكَ is the subject (doer) of the verb فَعَلَ (did/acted).

steps.m: to return, go back, or repeat

steps.n: Adding ا makes it a past tense verb

steps.m: with/by Aad (the tribe being punished by اللَّهِ)
steps.n: The word عَاد (name of an ancient tribe) gets بِ added at the start to mean 'with/by Aad', like saying what happened to them. The ٍ (tanween) at the end makes it a complete noun.

steps.m: Iram (name of an ancient powerful tribe)
steps.n: This is a proper noun referring to a legendary ancient Arabian tribe known for their power and tall buildings, mentioned in the Quran. It appears with ذَاتِ ٱلْعِمَادِ (of the pillars/columns) which describes their architectural achievements.

steps.m: Iram (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is the basic feminine form meaning 'possessor' or 'having'. It's used to describe something as having a quality or characteristic.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is describing what comes after it (العِمَادِ meaning 'the pillars'). Together they mean 'possessor of the pillars'.

steps.m: to prop up, support with pillars
steps.n: The root refers to the basic concept of supporting or holding something up
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he supported'
Changed from a verb (he supported) to a noun meaning many pillars by adding an alif and changing the vowels
Added 'al' to make it definite and kasra at the end to show it belongs to Iram (إِرَمَ)
This is the basic form of the feminine relative pronoun, used to connect descriptions to feminine nouns (like 'that' or 'which' in English)
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific. In this ayah, it refers to the city of Iram mentioned before, which is feminine in Arabic.
Changed the final ي to ى, which is just a writing style change that doesn't affect the meaning
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يُخْلَقْ) to show it did not happen in the past
Added sukun (ْ) at the end to show where the word stops
Adding damma on first letter and kasra on middle letter makes it passive voice, showing something was done to the subject rather than the subject doing it
Adding يُ at start shows present tense. The sukun marks on middle and end letters and removal of final vowel happen because لَمْ requires this form (called jussive).
Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'like' or 'similar'
Added ُ to show this word is the subject of يُخْلَقْ (was created) in this ayah
Added هَا meaning 'it/her' to show what is being compared. Here it refers back to the city (مَدِينَة) mentioned earlier
This is a preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'like of which was not created' with 'the cities/lands', showing where this creation was.
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This is the standard spelling of this common preposition.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple lands
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition فِى (in)

steps.m: Thamud (name of an ancient Arabian tribe)
steps.n: This is a proper noun referring to an ancient Arabian tribe mentioned in the Quran. They were known for carving houses into mountains.
Added a fatha at the end to show Thamud is the object being talked about in this sentence

steps.m: And Thamud (connected to previous nations mentioned)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this tribe to others mentioned before it
Starting with the demonstrative base that points to something
Adding ال at the beginning and ي at the end changes it from pointing at something to connecting parts of a sentence
Adding ين at the end makes it plural, referring to multiple people. Here it describes the people of Thamud mentioned in the previous word.
Adding the proper diacritical marks for pronunciation: shadda on ل, kasra under ذ, and fatha on ن. The initial alif is written with a waṣla mark showing it connects with the previous word.

steps.m: to cut through or traverse something

steps.n: The root letters ج-و-ب became جَابَ (he cut through) by adding fathas and changing و to ا - this is how many similar words change

steps.m: they cut through (subject of the verb)
steps.n: Added وا to show that a group of people did this action. This word describes what the people of Thamud did to the rocks in the valley.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the ص with shadda because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being carved through. The hamza at the start changed to a wasl form (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a place where water flows', which came to mean 'valley'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific valley

steps.m: in the valley (location where ثَمُودَ carved rocks)
steps.n: Added ب (meaning 'in/at') to show location - the valley where this happened

steps.m: Pharaoh - the title of ancient Egyptian kings
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language, so it doesn't have an Arabic root
The و at the beginning means 'and'

steps.m: and Pharaoh (object of وَٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا - those who denied)
steps.n: The fatḥa at the end shows Pharaoh is the object (the one being acted upon) - he was one of those who denied Allah's signs
This is a special word that shows possession or ownership. It doesn't come from a root - it's one of the basic building blocks of Arabic.
The word changes to ذِى because it's describing فِرْعَوْنَ (Pharaoh). It shows that Pharaoh possessed or owned something (in this case, الْأَوْتَادِ - the stakes/pegs).
Added short 'a' vowels (fathas) to turn the root letters into a simple noun meaning one peg
Changes from single peg to many pegs by adding أَ at the start and ا in the middle - like going from 'peg' to 'pegs' in English
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific pegs/stakes rather than any pegs/stakes

steps.m: of the pegs/stakes (possessed by فِرْعَوْنَ ذِى)
steps.n: Adding ِ shows this word belongs to or is connected to Pharaoh (فِرْعَوْنَ) through the word ذِى (possessing/having)
This is a relative pronoun used to describe or specify a group. It connects two parts of a sentence, like 'the ones who' or 'those who'
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific, like adding 'the' in English

steps.m: those who (subject of طَغَوْا۟ - they transgressed)
steps.n: Added نَ at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to طَغَوْا۟ (they transgressed) in the ayah, describing the people who did this action.

steps.m: to cross boundaries, to go beyond limits
he crossed boundaries (subject of فِى ٱلْبِلٰدِ)
steps.n: Added fathas to show this happened in the past. The ى at the end comes from the root letter ي. This word connects to فِى ٱلْبِلٰدِ showing where they crossed boundaries - in the lands.

steps.m: they crossed boundaries (subject of فِى ٱلْبِلٰدِ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together. The و replaces the ى because it's easier to pronounce with the وا ending.
Basic form of the preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it connects to 'the lands' to show where the transgression happened.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed the root into plural form by adding alif after second root letter

steps.m: the lands (object of طَغَوْا۟ فِى - they transgressed in)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and ِ at the end because it comes after the preposition في (in). The word describes what they transgressed in.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to cause something to increase'
Adding وا shows multiple people did the action

steps.m: so they made (something) increase (in فِيهَا ٱلْفَسَادَ)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this action happened as a result of something before it. In this ayah, they increased corruption (فَسَادَ) in it (فِيهَا).
This is a basic word that shows location or containment. It connects with the word الْفَسَادَ (corruption) to show where the corruption is happening.

steps.m: in it (referring to the land/earth mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها to show we're referring back to something feminine (the earth/land) that was mentioned before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Adding ا in the middle turns the verb into a noun meaning the act itself. This word is what is being increased (object of أَكْثَرُوا - they increased) in this ayah.
Adding ال makes it specific - we're talking about a particular corruption
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb before it (they increased). Also changed اَلْ to ٱلْ which is just a writing variation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes the pouring more intense

steps.m: and so he poured down forcefully (upon عَلَيْهِمْ)
steps.n: فَ shows this pouring happened as a result of something before. The word عَلَيْهِمْ after it shows who it was poured upon.

steps.m: upon (connected to فَصَبَّ - so poured upon)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something happening on top of something else. In this ayah, it connects to فَصَبَّ (poured) to show what was poured upon.
Added يْ as a connecting letter to prepare for attaching the pronoun

steps.m: upon them (object of فَصَبَّ - poured upon them)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (for a group). This shows who the action is happening to.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: The doubled ب with shadda shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly - a title especially used for Allah
Added ُ (damma) and كَ at the end. The damma shows this is the doer of the action صَبَّ (poured). The كَ means 'your' when speaking to one male person.
Changes from verb 'to whip' into the noun 'a whip'
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is receiving the action of صَبَّ (poured/unleashed). It's part of the phrase 'unleashed a whip of punishment'.
Added ا after the second root letter to turn the action of punishing into the punishment itself. This word follows سَوْط (whip) in the ayah, creating the phrase 'whip of punishment'.
The ٍ shows this word describes what kind of whip it is - a whip of punishment
This is the basic form of the word that shows something might happen
Adding the shadda changes this from showing possibility to showing certainty. This word makes the statement about 'your Lord' stronger and more definite.

steps.m: to care for and develop something to its full potential

steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show complete lordship
Added fatha at the end because إِنَّ requires the following word to take fatha
Added كَ at the end to show possession (your)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added مِ at start and ا after second root letter to create a noun meaning 'place where watching happens'
Added ال to make it specific
Added بِ meaning 'in/at' and ِ at the end because it follows a preposition

steps.m: surely in/at the place of watching (of رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added لَ for emphasis. This word describes how your Lord (رَبَّكَ) is always watching.
Starting with the basic word that means source/origin since this is a particle without a clear root
Adding ا changes this from meaning 'source' to being a word that introduces what we're talking about

steps.m: then as for (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning connects this to what came before, like saying 'then' or 'so'
Root meaning 'being social' became a noun for all humans, since humans are social beings
Changes from general 'mankind' to mean a single human being

steps.m: the human being (subject of يَقُولُ - he says)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this is the subject doing the action of saying in this verse
This word introduces a time condition that connects to what comes after it. It's used here to say 'when' something happens, specifically when Allah tests the person mentioned.
A versatile word that can be used as a relative pronoun or question word
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. In this ayah, مَا works with إِذَا to show when something happens - specifically when Allah tests someone.
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to show basic past tense meaning
Added Form VIII pattern (ٱ prefix and ت after first root letter) to show the action is being done reflexively or for oneself
Changed the final alif to an alif maqsurah with an overextension mark (ىٰ) due to weak letter rules

steps.m: he tested him (acted on by رَبُّهُ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'him' (هُ) to show who was being tested. This word is being acted upon by رَبُّهُ (his Lord) in this ayah.
Forms the noun 'lord' from the root
Root 'r-b-b' becomes 'lord/master' - the damma shows this is the doer/subject
Shows the Lord belongs to him. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb 'tests' (ابتلى) in this ayah.
Form IV verb making the meaning causative: 'to honor/be generous to'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and sukun on first root letter to make the action causative - showing someone making something happen

steps.m: and so he honored (connected to ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows from the previous action (testing) mentioned in the ayah
Added هُ as object pronoun to show who received the honoring
This is a special form used for praise
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The doubling of the middle letter shows doing something with extra effort or intensity
Added pronoun at the end to show who received the blessing
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this blessing with the previous honoring mentioned in the verse
This form turns the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of speaking'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed vowels because of the weak middle letter و

steps.m: and so he says (after being blessed by رَبُّهُۥ)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this happens as a result of what came before - the person saying this after being blessed by their Lord

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to create the noun 'lord'
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or possesses something
Added ى with a long vowel mark to show this means 'my'. This word is what the person says when speaking in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb form
Added 'أ' at the start to make it mean 'to make someone do something' - like changing 'honor' to 'make someone honor/treat with honor'
The suffix نِ is attached as a direct object pronoun meaning 'me'
This is a compound particle made up of multiple parts that work together to introduce a condition

steps.m: and as for (introduces a condition in إِذَا that follows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. The آ at the end makes the final sound longer. This word introduces a condition that is completed by إِذَا (when) in the next part.
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a specific point in time

steps.m: whenever (introducing the first part of the test mentioned in ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it more general and conditional. In this ayah, it introduces the first part of a test or trial that Allah gives someone.
In this ayah, مَا comes after أَ to form أَمَّا which combines with إِذَا to mean 'and as for when'. It helps introduce a new situation or case being discussed.
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowel marks (َ) and turning و into ا at the end
Added ا and ت to show the testing was done deliberately and with purpose (Form VIII verb pattern)
Added هُ (him) as the object of the testing
The هُ was already there in both words - it wasn't added. The main change is just spelling (alif maksura ى instead of regular alif ا)

steps.m: he measured/restricted (the provision mentioned in رِزْقَهُۥ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This refers to Allah restricting the provision (رِزْقَهُۥ) mentioned later in the verse.
The فَ shows this restriction was a consequence of the testing mentioned before (ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ)
This is a common preposition showing something is on top of or affecting something else. Here it connects to رِزْق (provision) in the ayah.

steps.m: upon him (referring to the person whose provision was restricted)
steps.n: The هِ shows who is being affected - 'him'
Root 'provide' became 'provision' - the fatha ending shows it's receiving the action of 'restricting'
The هُ shows this is 'his' provision. This word is what is being restricted by the verb قَدَرَ (to restrict/measure out) in the phrase 'restricted his provision'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: and so he says (responding to what happened before)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of Allah testing him by restricting his provision

steps.m: to nurture, care for, or develop

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to something after it
Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord. The person is saying this in the ayah.

steps.m: to be light, easy, or insignificant

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb. The middle و becomes ا because it's a weak letter.
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'cause someone to be insignificant' = 'humiliate'
Added نِ at the end to show 'me' as the object (the person being humiliated). The ا is written as ٰ when نِ is added.
This is the base part of the emphatic particle that shows strong disagreement
Adding ل with shadda makes the word stronger, showing complete rejection

steps.m: No indeed! (strongly rejecting what came before بَل لَّا تُكْرِمُونَ ٱلْيَتِيمَ - 'But no, you do not honor the orphan')
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes this a complete emphatic particle that strongly rejects what was said before. In this ayah, it's rejecting wrong behavior and introducing the criticism that follows about not honoring orphans.
This is a particle that strongly disagrees with what came before (كَلَّا - 'No!') and introduces the real situation. Here it's saying 'No! Rather, the truth is you don't honor the orphan.'
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'

steps.m: nay!/certainly not! (negating what came before كَلَّا بَل)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger and more emphatic. Here it strongly rejects or corrects what was mentioned before, followed by بَل (rather/but) to introduce the correct statement.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb is acting on يَتِيمَ (orphan) in this ayah.
Added vowels and ي to change from action to describing someone affected by the action

steps.m: the orphan (object of تُكْرِمُونَ - you all honor)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the start to talk about a specific orphan. This orphan is the one that 'you all' are not honoring in the verse.

steps.m: the orphan (receiving the action of not being honored)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this orphan is receiving the action of not being honored
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before it
Added fatha on lam to help with pronunciation
Basic meaning of encouraging or urging someone to do something
Adding vowel marks to make it a proper past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to mean 'you' and doubled the middle letter ض to show strong urging
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the urging. This verb connects to 'feeding of the poor' (طَعَامِ ٱلْمِسْكِينِ) in the ayah with عَلَىٰ.
This is the basic form of the word that means something being high or elevated

Adding ى changes this from a concept of height into a preposition showing something is on top of or about something else. Here it connects to the word طَعَامِ (food) to show the relationship 'upon the food'.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound, not as a 'y' sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he fed/tasted' (verb) to 'food' (noun) by adding an alif after the first letter. Like changing 'eat' to 'food' in English.

steps.m: food of (possessed by المِسْكِينِ - the needy)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to المِسْكِينِ (the needy) in a possession relationship, meaning 'food of the needy'
Basic past tense form of the verb

steps.m: needy person (one who has no settlement/resources)
steps.n: Added مِ at start and ي between ك and ن to create a noun for someone who lacks settlement/resources
Added ٱلْ to specify a particular needy person and ِ to show this is the object being fed in the phrase 'feeding the needy'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and'. This verb is connected to ٱلتُّرَاثَ (inheritance) which is what is being eaten.

steps.m: to inherit or receive something from someone who died
steps.n: This is the basic meaning showing what happens when someone passes away and leaves something behind

steps.m: inheritance (the thing that was left behind)
steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun meaning the actual thing that was inherited
Adds ال to make it specific and َ to show it's the object being eaten in the phrase 'and you devour the inheritance'
Adding ال makes it specific
Changed from basic verb root to noun meaning 'the act of eating'
Added tanween alif to show this describes how the eating is done in تَأْكُلُونَ (you eat). The ًا ending shows it's describing the manner of eating.

steps.m: to gather up completely or consume entirely

steps.n: Added fatha vowels and doubled the م to show completeness of action
Added ا at the end to change it from a verb (gathering) into a word that describes how something is done (completely/entirely). It tells us they eat the inheritance in a complete/total way.
Added shadda on first letter for extra emphasis, showing how greedy and complete the consumption is
Changed the ending mark to show it describes how completely something was done
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense and added person marker
Added ونَ to change from 'you/she loves' to 'you all love' - shows multiple people are doing the loving
Added وَ to connect this to the previous statement. This word describes how people love wealth (مَالَ) mentioned right after it.
Added vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to the root letters to make the word for 'wealth'
The weak letter و changes to ا in this pattern
Added ال to make it specific. This word is what is being loved in the phrase 'and you love wealth'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being loved

steps.m: to love deeply from the heart

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to love' to the noun 'love' by adding damma
Added ًا (tanween fatha) to show this is 'a love' and make it the direct object - the thing being loved
Added a shadda (double letter) to make it mean 'very abundant'
Added tanween alif to make it describe how something is done. This word describes how they love wealth (حُبًّا) in the ayah.
Added fatha and shadda to make the basic negative particle stronger

steps.m: No indeed! (strongly rejecting what comes in إِذَا دُكَّتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ)
steps.n: Added the long alif at the end to add even more emphasis to the rejection. This word is strongly denying something about the following phrase 'when the earth is crushed'.
This is the basic form meaning 'when' or 'at the time'. It's used to talk about specific times.

steps.m: when (introducing the future event of the earth being crushed)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes this from just 'when' to specifically talking about a future event. Here it introduces what will happen when the earth is crushed on the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: to crush or level completely flat

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive voice with damma at start and shadda in middle to show something received intense crushing action

steps.m: was crushed completely (referring to الأَرْضُ - the earth)
steps.n: Added ت to show this refers to the feminine noun الأَرْضُ (the earth) that appears after this word in the ayah
Basic root letters shaped into the common noun 'earth' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: the earth (subject of being crushed in دُكَّتِ)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific (the earth) and ُ to show it's the subject being crushed in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (crushing) to a noun meaning the action itself - like going from 'crushed' to 'crushing'

steps.m: with complete crushing (describes how دُكَّتِ happened to ٱلْأَرْضُ)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word describes how the earth was crushed, relating to the verb دُكَّتِ (was crushed) which acts on ٱلْأَرْضُ (the earth)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
The doubling of the middle letter indicates intensity or repetition of the crushing action

steps.m: completely crushed/leveled (describing how دُكَّتِ happens to ٱلْأَرْضُ)
steps.n: The ًا ending changes this from a verb into a word that describes how thoroughly and completely the crushing/leveling happens to the earth
Added fatha marks and connected the letters. The middle letter ي changes to ا which is common in words like this.
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'. This word is connected to رَبُّكَ (your Lord) who is the one doing the coming.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures in a complete and perfect way

steps.m: one who nurtures completely (doer of جَآءَ/came)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of coming (جَآءَ) in this ayah
Adds كَ to show this Lord belongs to or is connected to you
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'angel'
Added the definite article ال to specify this particular angel
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase (وَجَآءَ رَبُّكَ - and your Lord came)
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action of coming, along with 'your Lord' in the previous phrase
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle root letter indicates intensification of the base meaning
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the angels came - in rows (related to جَآءَ meaning 'came' in this ayah)

steps.m: to line up or arrange in rows

steps.n: Doubled the middle letter to emphasize the completeness of the row

steps.m: in a row (describing how the angels came)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the angels came - they came arranged in a row. This is connected to the verb جَآءَ (came) in this ayah.
Connected letters with vowels. The middle letter ي changes to ا which is common for these types of words.
Adding kasra (ِ) on first letter changes it to passive voice

steps.m: and was brought (connecting to what comes after)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to what follows it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used as a time expression

steps.m: on that day (when Hell is brought near)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ which points to a specific time being discussed. Here it refers to the Day of Judgment when Hell is brought near, as mentioned in the ayah.
The double kasra (ٍۭ) adds emphasis and makes the word flow better in Arabic
This is a proper noun referring to Hell. It comes from a non-Arabic origin so it has no Arabic root.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being brought in the sentence
Added بِ at the beginning meaning 'with'. This shows Hell is being brought along with something else mentioned in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Added ئِذٍ to mean 'at that time', making it mean 'on that day'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show deep self-reflection
Changed to present tense with يَ and ُ. This happens 'on that Day' (يَوْمَئِذٍ) mentioned in the ayah.
Root meaning 'being social' became a noun for all humans by changing the first letter and adding vowel marks
Adding ان changes it from humans as a species to a single human being

steps.m: the human being (subject of يَتَذَكَّرُ - remembers/takes heed)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and ُ at end to show this is the one doing the remembering in this verse

steps.m: how/from where (questioning the ability to remember)
steps.n: This is a question word used here to express doubt about the possibility of remembering or benefiting from the reminder at that time

steps.m: and how/from where (questioning the ability to remember)
steps.n: The و connects this question to the previous statement about remembering, emphasizing the futility of remembrance at that time
This is a preposition showing possession or purpose

steps.m: for him/to him (referring to الْإِنسٰنُ - the human)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to. This refers back to الْإِنسٰنُ (the human) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'to remember' into 'a reminder' - like changing 'run' to 'a running'
Adds the definite article 'the'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to common past tense form with alif due to weak middle letter
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ vowels. The middle ا changes back to و in present tense.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make the basic word
Added يٰ to express strong emotion of regret (this person is speaking about their past deeds)

steps.m: oh, if only I (had) (subject expressing regret about قَدَّمْتُ - what they sent ahead)
steps.n: Added نِى at the end to show this is about the speaker themselves. This connects to the next word قَدَّمْتُ (I sent ahead) to express regret about past actions.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Adding the shadda (doubled middle letter) changes it from 'going forward himself' to 'sending something/someone else forward'

steps.m: I sent ahead (saying يَقُولُ - he says)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past. This word appears in the context where someone is speaking (يَقُولُ - he says) about what they did.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'life' by adding alif after second letter and ة at end
Added لِ at start to mean 'for/to', with kasra at end because of لِ
Added ى at the end to show possession ('my'). The ة changes to ت when connected to ى
Adding ي at the end makes it possessive ('my')
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun 'day'
Adding ئِذ points to a specific day being talked about
Adding tanwin shows this word describes when something happens

steps.m: so on that day (connects to لَّا يُعَذِّبُ - no one will punish)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happens as a result of something before it. In this ayah, it connects to the following statement about punishment.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُعَذِّبُ (punishes) into 'does not punish'
The shadda makes the negation stronger, emphasizing that NO ONE will punish like His punishment
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong punishment
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and ُ at end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after second letter to make it into a noun meaning the result of the action
Added هُ to show the punishment belongs to him. This word is what's being acted upon in relation to the verb يُعَذِّبُ (punishes).

steps.m: anyone (who will be punished like His punishment)
steps.n: The tanween (ٌ) makes this indefinite, meaning 'anyone' rather than a specific person. This word is part of a negative statement saying no one will punish like Allah's punishment.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُوثِقُ that comes after it, meaning 'no one will bind/chain'
The و adds 'and' to connect this negation with what came before. Together with the verb after it, it means 'and no one will bind/chain'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word is being negated in the ayah - no one will bind.

steps.m: to tie up, to bind securely
steps.n: Starting from the same root as the previous word
Forms a noun for the physical thing used to bind - like a rope, chain, or fetter

steps.m: something used for tying/binding (being acted upon)
steps.n: Shows this is what's being affected by the action in يُوثِقُ (binds/ties up)

steps.m: his binding/chain (being acted upon by يُوثِقُ - binds/ties up)
steps.n: Adds 'his' to show who the binding belongs to. This whole word is what's being affected by the action in يُوثِقُ (binds/ties up)
Connected the root letters and added fathas to make it into a noun meaning 'one'
Added two dots (tanween) to show this is the subject doing the action - meaning 'anyone' or 'no one' in this ayah

steps.m: to come, to arrive, to appear

steps.n: The root أ-ي-ي is not related to the question word أَيّ. The word أَيّ is a separate interrogative word meaning 'which' or 'what' - it's not derived from 'to come'.
Added feminine marker ة to make the word refer to feminine things
Added calling particle يٰٓ to show direct address
Added 'u' sound at the end to show we're directly calling out to someone
Added ها to complete the emphatic form of address

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, to be a self

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning soul or self. This word is being directly addressed in the ayah with يٰٓأَيَّتُهَا (O you).
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the soul' instead of just 'soul'
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is the subject
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at the start to show who experiences the peace, and changed the middle vowels to show an ongoing state
The doubled ن (shown by ّ) makes the feeling of peace stronger

steps.m: feminine one who feels deep peace
steps.n: Adding ة makes this word feminine

steps.m: the female one who feels deep peace
steps.n: Adding ال makes the word definite, changing it from 'a peaceful one' to 'the peaceful one'

steps.m: O soul that feels complete peace (being addressed in يٰٓأَيَّتُهَا ٱلنَّفْسُ)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this describes the soul (ٱلنَّفْسُ) being called out to with يٰٓأَيَّتُهَا (O you)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he returned' to 'return!' by adding i- at start and changing the vowels to make it a command to one person

steps.m: return! (command to one female) (to رَبِّكِ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show we're speaking to a female. This word is connected to رَبِّكِ (your Lord) in the ayah.
Changed the ي to ى with a long vowel mark to show it's stretched when reading
Basic preposition showing direction or movement toward something. In this ayah, it connects 'return' with 'your Lord'
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to show how it's pronounced
Added small alif (ٰ) above the final letter to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who does this action completely
Changed to show possession - like saying 'my lord' instead of just 'lord'

steps.m: your Lord (addressing a female, after إلى/to)
steps.n: Added كِ at the end to show this is addressing a female (the peaceful soul from previous ayah)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The و changes to ي because it's easier to pronounce.
Changed from past tense 'was pleased' to active participle 'one who is pleased' by adding ا after ر
Added ة to make it feminine, since it describes a feminine soul (نفس) returning to Allah
Added ً (tanween) to show this describes the state of the soul as it returns to Allah
Added vowels to make past tense. The root letter و changes to ي in the middle of the word.
Added مَ at the start to show someone/something is receiving the action (being made content), rather than doing it. The doubled ي at the end shows it's complete/permanent.
Added ة to make it feminine - like saying 'satisfied' vs 'satisfied (feminine)'
Added tanween (two fathas) to show it's indefinite - like saying 'a satisfied one' instead of 'the satisfied one'
Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed from 'he entered' to a command meaning 'you enter!' by adding U sound at start and changing vowels
Added ي with kasra to make it feminine command
Added فَ meaning 'so/then' and changed hamza to wasl form. This command is connected to the following phrase فِى عِبٰدِى (among My servants)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'enter' (فَٱدْخُلِى) with 'My servants' (عِبٰدِى), showing where to enter.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed from basic root meaning 'serve/worship' into noun meaning 'servant' by adding vowels
Changes from 'abd' to 'ibaad' to make it mean many servants instead of one
Changed to show ownership - like saying 'servants of' instead of just 'servants'
Added first person possessive pronoun to show ownership
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command to one person
Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier
Added ى to show we're speaking to a female
Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this command with what comes before it. This word is connected to جَنَّتِى (my garden) which is what is being entered.
Added the basic past tense form by connecting letters and adding shadda

steps.m: a garden (a place full of covering trees)
steps.n: Adds ة to make it mean 'a garden' - a place with lots of trees that cover/hide what's underneath

steps.m: my garden (mentioned after وَٱدْخُلِى - and enter)
steps.n: The ي at the end means 'my'
